data.title: Bull Case for India Hospitality Sector Backed by Luxury, Corporate Travel Demand — Market Talk
data.description: The bull case for India's hospitality sector is backed by luxury and corporate travel demand, Nomura's Akash Gupta says in a research report. The sector is in a structural upcycle, given a widening demand-supply gap in luxury hospitality and resilient growth in corporate travel, the analyst says. This should sustain strong average-daily-rate-led growth of the sector. Nomura initiates coverage of Leela Palaces Hotels & Resorts with a buy rating and a target price of 510.00 rupees and Chalet Hotels with a neutral rating and target price of 860.00 rupees. Meanwhile, the brokerage lowers the target price of ITC Hotels' stock to 210.00 rupees from 230.00 rupees with an unchanged buy rating. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on April 22
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Wednesday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

March consumer price index (ZACPIY=ECI)

February retail sales data (ZARET=ECI)

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand weakened on Tuesday as investors weighed geopolitical tensions linked to the Iran war, while investors digested weaker domestic business confidence data and warnings from the central bank about inflation risks linked to the conflict.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was down 1.47%.

GLOBAL MARKETS

U.S. stock futures rose and the dollar wavered on Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would indefinitely extend the Iran ceasefire, keeping sentiment buoyed, although with the Strait of Hormuz still closed, oil prices stayed near $100.

GOLD

Gold prices firmed on Wednesday as lower oil prices, following a U.S. extension of a ceasefire with Iran, eased fears of an inflation spike and prolonged high interest rates.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

- - - -

Some of the main stories out in the South African press:

NEWS24

- Upcoming elections are funded, despite R1.6bn budget deficit, says IEC

- Draft forex rules ditch some apartheid-era restrictions

data.title: Dettol maker Reckitt misses market expectations for Q1 sales growth on weak U.S., Europe
data.description:
Dettol soap maker Reckitt reported a 1.3% growth in like-for-like net revenue for its core business on Wednesday, missing market expectations due to weak pricing in the U.S. and soft demand in Europe.

The company, which maintained its 2026 outlook, was expected to report revenue growth of 2.9% for its core unit on a like-for-like basis in the three months ended March, according to analysts in a company-compiled poll.

data.title: Jefferies Keeps Betting on Two Fed Rate Cuts — Market Talk
data.description: Jefferies affirms its expectations of two interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, global economist Mohit Kumar says after Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing on Tuesday. Warsh supported lower rates and smaller balance sheet, broadly in line with market expectations, Kumar says. "Our view remains that a Warsh led Fed would be more dovish on the rates front," he says. "We maintain our call for two rate cuts from the Fed, though we acknowledge that the two cuts may not happen this year." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)
